name: Netherlands
description: The Netherlands is a forward-thinking nation with world-class universities. It offers hundreds of English-taught bachelor's and master's programs, making it a top choice for international students in Europe.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: The Fees for Visa and residence permit application is usually included in the University Tuition fees package which is approx. € 228
tuitionFees.postgraduate: One year living expense must be shown – EUR 15,000 + Balance tuition fees.
tuitionFees.phd: Often fully funded as a paid PhD employee position

requirements: Proof of financial means (€11,000+ per year)
Authorization for Temporary Stay (MVV) if required

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 16 hours/week; full-time in June, July, August
workPermit.postStudy: 1-year Orientation Year (Zoekjaar) visa for graduates of Dutch or top-100 QS universities

languageRequirements.notes: Many programs taught in English; Dutch not required

topUniversities.name: The Hague University of Applied Sciences through The Hague Pathway College
University of Twente through Twente Pathway College

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Netherlands?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Netherlands is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Netherlands?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
